metadata.instruction: Set DuckDuckGo as the default search engine on Chrome
metadata.context: I prefer a privacy-focused search engine and want to use DuckDuckGo by default.

l3.rationale: Clicked the Google Chrome list item to launch Chrome.

l4.rationale: Launched Google Chrome from Program Manager, opening the browser application.

l5.rationale: Launches the browser from the app list so the user can start changing Chrome's default search engine.

l3.rationale: Moved the pointer toward the address/search bar.

l4.rationale: Navigated through Chrome's browser controls and menus, including opening the menu, incognito window, tabs, and scrolling to reach Settings.

l5.rationale: Uses Chrome navigation, repeated search-box focus, menu actions, and scrolling to get to the search engine settings area.

actions.window_title: Settings - Manage search engines and site search - Google Chrome

focused_element.name: Manage search engines and site search subpage back button

element_under_cursor.name: Settings - Manage search engines and site search

l3.rationale: Moved the pointer to the back button on the subpage.

l4.rationale: Opened DuckDuckGo's more actions menu and selected Make default to finalize DuckDuckGo as the default search engine.

l5.rationale: Uses button and menu-item selection to confirm DuckDuckGo as the chosen default search engine.

l5.rationale: Uses button and menu-item selection to confirm DuckDuckGo as the chosen default search engine.
